rationale: The citing paper uses Wang, Gu, and Dong (2013) for a specific conceptual role in its theory, not just as a general background citation. In the background and hypothesis development, it cites this study as evidence that monitoring capability affects opportunism hazards and the enforceability value of monitoring. In the discussion, the authors explicitly position their contribution as building on that prior monitoring-capability insight by showing a different consequence in their MCS/IIOT setting, namely that more monitoring can intensify disputes.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Wang et al. (2013) to motivate that monitoring capability is consequential for channel governance, then develops this idea in a different industrial systems context. It contributes a new implication relative to that line of work by showing a monitoring-penalty pathway where increased monitoring is associated with greater disputes.
